basic_details.job_title: MPPSC Scientific Officer Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 81 Posts
basic_details.short_information: Madhya Pradesh Public Service Commission (MPPSC) has released an official notification for the recruitment of 81 Scientific Officer Posts. Interested and eligible candidates can apply Online through the official MPPSC website. The last date to submit the application form is 20-05-2026. In this article, you’ll find the MPPSC Scientific Officer Posts recruitment details, including eligibility criteria, age limit, salary structure, selection process, application steps, and direct links to the official notification and Online application form.

application_fee.details: Fee payment through Credit Card, Debit Card, or Net Banking. Fee can also be paid at M.P. Online authorized kiosks in cash. Late Application Fee (after 20.05.2026 to 27.05.2026): ₹3,000/- additional. Late Application Fee (after 28.05.2026 to 28.10.2026): ₹25,000/- additional. Error correction fee: ₹50/- per correction (up to 28.10.2026). Fee once paid will not be refunded except in the case of advertisement cancellation by the Commission, in which case fee will be returned online to the candidate's account.

age_limit.details: Minimum Age: 21 Years Maximum Age: 40 Years Age is computed as on 01 January 2027. Only the date of birth as recorded in the 10th (High School) marksheet will be accepted. Candidates must mention the same name, father's name, and date of birth in the online application as in their High School marksheet. Age Relaxation: SC/ST/OBC/Divyangjan/Government Employees/Nagar Sainik/Women (Unreserved/Reserved): 5 years relaxation in upper age limit as per MP Government order Saa.Pra.Vib. No. Ci.3-8/2016/1/3 dated 04 July 2019. Ex-Servicemen: Age reduced by length of defence service; resultant age must not exceed upper age limit by more than 3 years. Intercaste married couple (Anusuchit Jati-Savarna) under Promotion Scheme: 5 years. Vikram Puraskar awardee sportspersons: 5 years. In no case should the maximum age exceed 45 years after all relaxations.

qualification.education_qualification: Post Graduate (M.Sc. Second Division) in Chemistry/Physics /Botany/Zoology/Biochemistry/Microbiology/Biotechnology/Forensic Biotechnology/Genetics/Molecular Biology/Bioscience/Life Science/Forensic Science; AND Graduate with prescribed subjects in Biology and Chemistry streams
qualification.details: Essential Educational Qualification (Post Graduate): Post Graduate (M.Sc.) in Second Division in any of the following: Physics, Chemistry, Botany (Vanaspati Vigyan), Zoology (Jantु/Praani Vigyan), Biochemistry, Microbiology, Biotechnology, Forensic Biotechnology, Genetics, Molecular Biology, Bioscience, Life Science, or Forensic Science. Essential Educational Qualification (Graduate – both (a) and (b) mandatory): (a) Any one of: Botany, Zoology, Bioscience, Life Science, Biotechnology, Microbiology, or Forensic Biology. (b) Any one of: Chemistry or Biochemistry/Forensic Chemistry. Department: Madhya Pradesh Shasan, Home Department (Grih Vibhag). Post Grade: Rajpatrit II Shreni (Gazetted Class II). Post Status: Temporary (Asthaayi). Employment Registration: Candidates from other states are exempted from registration in Employment Exchange as per High Court order dated 08.03.2022 in Writ Petition No. 5096/2022. MP domicile candidates' live Employment Exchange registration is not required at the time of application, but must be submitted by the last date of document submission before the interview. MP domicile candidates serving in Government or Non-Government service are exempt from Employment Exchange registration on production of No Objection Certificate (Government employees) or Service Certificate (Non-Government employees). All qualifications must be completed on or before the last date of application i.e. 20.05.2026.

vacancy_details.Post Name: Vaigyanik Adhikari Jeev Vigyan (Scientific Officer Biology)
Vaigyanik Adhikari Jeev Vigyan (Scientific Officer Physics)
Vaigyanik Adhikari Jeev Vigyan (Scientific Officer Chemistry)

salary_details.pay_scale: Pay Scale: ₹15,600 – ₹39,100 + Grade Pay ₹5,400 Per Month
salary_details.details: Pay Scale: ₹15,600 – ₹39,100 Per Month Grade Pay: ₹5,400/- Post Grade: Rajpatrit II Shreni (Gazetted Class II) Other allowances as applicable per Government rules for Madhya Pradesh State services.

selection_process: Written Examination (OMR Based): Objective type questions on OMR sheet will be conducted. Negative Marking (Rnnatmak Mulyankan) is applicable. Exam centres: Indore

Jabalpur and Gwalior. Exam Plan and Syllabus will be published on the Commission's website www.mppsc.mp.gov.in in due course. Candidates up to a maximum of three times the notified vacancies (category-wise) will be shortlisted for interview based on written exam marks. Interview (Viva Voce / Saakshaatkar): Candidates shortlisted from the written examination will be called for interview. Interview total marks: 100. Minimum qualifying marks for Unreserved category: 41; for Reserved categories and Divyangjan: 31. Final selection will be based on the merit order of aggregate marks (written examination + interview). Candidates absent from interview will be considered ineligible for selection. If total applicants are less than 500: Selection will be based on interview only. Candidates will be shortlisted directly for interview as per the following ratio: 1 post – up to 12 candidates; 2–3 posts – up to 24; 4–6 posts – up to 36; 7–9 posts – up to 48; 10 posts and above – up to 50 candidates or 5 times the vacancies. Post-Answer Key Process: Provisional answer key will be published on the Commission's website after the exam. Objections can be submitted within 05 days at ₹150/- per question (plus ₹40/- portal charge). Final answer key will be published after expert committee review. No objections will be entertained after publication of the final answer key.
how_to_apply: Online applications must be submitted at www.mppsc.mp.gov.in. Applications through any other means will not be accepted.
Before filling the form, prepare a recent passport-size colour photograph scan and signature scan file as per instructions in the notification.
Fee can be paid via Credit Card, Debit Card, Net Banking, or at M.P. Online authorized kiosks in cash.

After filling the form, a popup window will display basic information (name, parents' names, date of birth, category, gender) for verification. If any error is found, click Cancel to go back and correct. Click OK to submit. After successful submission, the application number will be generated (status will be Unpaid until fee is paid).

After submitting, click "Proceed for Payment" to complete fee payment. After successful payment, the application will be received and "Payment Done" will be displayed. Take a printout of the application form and preserve the application number for future reference.

Error Correction Facility: Online application correction can be done from 26.04.2026 to 22.05.2026 (till 12:00 midnight) at ₹50/- per correction. Applications submitted with late fee: corrections allowed up to 28.10.2026 at ₹50/- per correction.
Category/Gender/Date of Birth cannot be changed after the error correction period ends.
Candidates must ensure their signature is consistent across all documents; mismatch of signatures on various documents will lead to cancellation of candidature.

No documents need to be uploaded at the time of online application. However, before the interview, attested self-certified documents must be submitted to the Commission as per the detailed checklist in Annexure/Parishisht-01 of the notification.
For any query or complaint regarding online application filling: Helpline – 0755-6720220; Email – [email protected].
important_instructions: All amendments/instructions issued by the Government from time to time regarding age, domicile, employment exchange registration, reservation etc. will be incorporated into the final selection result. The syllabus is attached in Bhaag-"H" of the notification and will be published on the Commission's website www.mppsc.mp.gov.in. Cut-off marks of written exam and interview will not be disclosed prior to final selection. If a computer or clerical error is noticed after declaration of result, the Commission reserves the right to correct it. Reserved category seats (SC/ST/OBC) and age relaxation benefits are available only to Madhya Pradesh domicile candidates. Candidates from other states will be considered as Unreserved. In any reserved category, if no eligible female candidate is available, the reserved seats will be filled by male candidates of the same category. Divyangjan (Persons with Disabilities) candidates must attach the latest valid disability certificate issued by the District Medical Board constituted as per Ministry notification F-8-1-Satreh-Medi-2, dated 09.01.2009. Disability certificate issued after 01.06.2021 must be from the UID portal http://www.swavlambancard.gov.in. Manually issued certificates after that date will be invalid. Minimum 40% permanent disability is required to claim reservation and other benefits. Candidates who are in Government service (temporary, daily wage, contractual, or under public undertakings) must submit an Undertaking that they have informed their Head of Department/office about applying for this post. All information published regarding this recruitment notification including result will be available only on www.mppsc.mp.gov.in. Commission will not communicate through Email/letters/phone individually. EWS (Economically Weaker Section) certificate valid for financial year 2026-27 must be held by the candidate by the last date of online application i.e. 20.05.2026. OBC candidates must ensure they are not in the Creamy Layer. Non-Creamy Layer declaration as per the prescribed proforma must be submitted. Final selection result for OBC reservation is subject to the pending court cases SLP 8764/2023 and WP5596/2024 and related matters. The result will be declared in Main Part (87%) and Provisional Part (13%) as per MP Govt. order dated 29.09.2022. Disqualifications under MP Civil Services Rules 1961: Candidates convicted of crimes against women, candidates with more than 2 living children (where one was born on or after 26 January 2001) are not eligible for appointment.

faq.question: Q1. What is the total number of vacancies in MPPSC Scientific Officer Biology Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 25 vacancies have been notified under Advertisement No. 07/2026 dated 16.03.2026 for the post of Vaigyanik Adhikari Jeev Vigyan (Scientific Officer Biology) under Madhya Pradesh Shasan, Home Department.

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to apply for MPPSC Scientific Officer Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date for online application is 20-05-2026. Applications with late fee of ₹3,000/- can be submitted up to 27-05-2026, and with late fee of ₹25,000/- up to 28-10-2026.

faq.question: Q3. What is the salary for MPPSC Scientific Officer post 2026?
faq.answer: The pay scale is ₹15,600 – ₹39,100 with Grade Pay ₹5,400/- per month. This is a Gazetted Class II (Rajpatrit II Shreni) temporary post under the Home Department, Madhya Pradesh.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for MPPSC Scientific Officer Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The minimum age is 21 years and the maximum age is 40 years as on 01.01.2027. Age relaxation of 5 years is available for SC/ST/OBC/Divyangjan/Government Employees/Nagar Sainik/Women as per MP Government order. In no case should the final age exceed 45 years after all relaxations.

faq.question: Q5. What is the application fee for MPPSC Scientific Officer 2026?
faq.answer: The fee is ₹250/- for MP domicile SC/ST/OBC (Non-Creamy Layer)/EWS/Divyangjan candidates and ₹500/- for all other categories and candidates from outside Madhya Pradesh. The fee can be paid via Credit Card, Debit Card, Net Banking, or at M.P. Online authorized kiosks.

faq.question: Q6. What educational qualification is required for MPPSC Scientific Officer Biology 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must have a Post Graduate degree (M.Sc.) in Second Division in Botany/Zoology/Biochemistry/Microbiology/Biotechnology/Forensic Biotechnology/Genetics/Molecular Biology/Bioscience/Life Science/Forensic Science, AND a Graduate degree with (a) a Biology-related subject and (b) Chemistry or Biochemistry/Forensic Chemistry.

faq.question: Q7. What is the selection process for MPPSC Scientific Officer Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Selection consists of two stages: a Written Examination (OMR-based, objective type, with negative marking) conducted at Indore, Bhopal, Jabalpur and Gwalior, followed by an Interview (Viva Voce) for shortlisted candidates. Final merit is based on aggregate marks of both stages. If total applicants are fewer than 500, selection will be based on interview only.

faq.question: Q8. What is the exam date for MPPSC Scientific Officer 2026?
faq.answer: The examination is scheduled on 22-11-2026. Admit cards will be available for download from 12-11-2026 on the Commission's website www.mppsc.mp.gov.in.

faq.question: Q9. Is there negative marking in the MPPSC Scientific Officer Exam 2026?
faq.answer: Yes, Negative Marking (Rnnatmak Mulyankan) is applicable in the written examination. Candidates are advised to carefully review the exam plan and marking scheme which will be published on the Commission's website www.mppsc.mp.gov.in.

faq.question: Q10. Are Divyangjan (Persons with Disabilities) candidates eligible for MPPSC Scientific Officer 2026?
faq.answer: No. As per the official notification, Divyangjan (Persons with Disabilities) category candidates are NOT eligible to apply for the post of Vaigyanik Adhikari Jeev Vigyan (Scientific Officer Biology) in this recruitment.
